text: Abstract Bayesian Networks (BNs) have been widely applied to various fields for uncertainty reasoning over the last few decades. As many complex real-world problems need to investigate the effects of hypothetical interventions or decisions, BNs have emerged as a powerful tool to model, analyze and manage complex systems. While BNs are a very valuable representation of uncertain knowledge, one major open issue is how to determine a suitable graph structure for a model when one has some prior causal belief on the relationships between variables. Therefore, many approaches have been designed in order to automatically deduce a graph from data, to construct a BN from prior knowledge, or even a combination of these two approaches. This paper gives a survey on the combinatoric algorithms dedicated to learning BN structures from data, including 74 algorithms that are exemplified, well-known and that belong to the state-of-the-art on this topic. We describe for each of them their main procedures in a unified presentation framework, which enables us to highlight their common or differing aspects. We also study how these works evaluate the performance of the algorithms they propose and how reliability is ensured when reporting on such comparisons, especially in presence of high noisiness in the data or when incorporating prior knowledge.
explanation: The passage is highly polished and reads like a survey abstract, but the generic phrasing and an exact but broad claim (74 algorithms) can be a hallmark of AI-generated or heavily edited text.
